objective: Guarantee connector data availability across all Chromium browsers (Dia/Arc, Edge, Brave) by hardening cookie injection, session detection, and fetch strategies

content: Strategy: 3 modules to create/modify — (1) browser-compat.ts diagnostic module in Core, (2) harden cookie-rules.ts with fallback strategies, (3) harden base.connector.ts fetchJSON/fetchHTML with explicit cookie injection for non-Chrome browsers
reason: Collective and Free-Work fail on Dia because credentials:include does not forward cookies from extension context on non-Chrome Chromium browsers. declarativeNetRequest cookie injection is the reliable cross-browser strategy.

content: Free-Work is a public API requiring no auth, but fetch may fail due to CORS/Origin header issues on non-Chrome browsers. Add Origin header rewriting for free-work.com similar to collective/lehibou pattern.
reason: Dia may send different Origin header from extension context causing API rejection

content: Add runtime browser detection in Core (pure function, no I/O) that classifies browser capabilities. Shell uses this to decide which cookie/fetch strategy to apply.
reason: FC&IS compliance: detection logic is pure (takes userAgent string), strategy selection is Shell

description: Unused import: type BrowserInfo imported on line 21 but never referenced in the file

reason: Minor violation: unused type BrowserInfo import in collective.connector.ts
All tests pass (605/605), TypeScript clean, no violations remaining

subject: FreeWork Dia/Chrome cross-browser fix using explicit Accept-Language header
concerns: The root-cause diagnosis (Accept-Language locale sensitivity) is strong and likely correct, but forcing French may hide locale-coupling instead of documenting it as an API contract.

Setting Accept-Language manually is a forbidden/safelisted-but-user-agent-controlled header in some environments; fetch may ignore or normalize it on some browsers, so behavior should be verified in-browser and covered by tests or health checks.

The connector still silently breaks pagination when page 1 returns 0 valid missions from an otherwise valid Hydra response; this is okay for now but would benefit from stronger diagnostics when totalItems=0 unexpectedly on public endpoints.
recommendations: Keep the explicit French locale fix, but add a dedicated unit/integration regression test or health-check assertion for FreeWork locale-dependent behavior.
Consider centralizing locale-sensitive API headers in BaseConnector or connector metadata so cross-browser behavior is easier to reason about and reuse.
If possible, log the effective browser/locale once in DEV and surface a connector error when a public endpoint that normally has inventory returns 0 unexpectedly across multiple attempts.
